subject: Human Resources
title: Re-Engaging Lost Employees
description: A fast breakdown of Re-Engaging Lost Employees for builders.

hook.text: She was the top performer three years ago. Now she does the minimum. Her manager finally asks: 'What changed?'

buildup.text: Re-engagement starts with curiosity, not judgment—understanding what went wrong before trying to fix it.

discovery.text: Common fixable causes: role mismatch, stalled growth, broken trust with a manager, or unacknowledged contributions.

twist.text: Not everyone can be re-engaged. Sometimes the best outcome is helping them find a role where they'll truly thrive.

climax.text: A genuine conversation—'What do you need to do your best work?'—re-engages more people than any program.

punchline.text: Before writing someone off, ask what went wrong.

quiz.question: What is the first step to re-engaging a disengaged employee?
options: Asking with curiosity what changed, not judging
